text: When you work with APIs, you often send HTTP requests to a server to get or send data. Most APIs use JSON format to exchange information, which is easy to read and process. Many APIs require authentication, usually with an API key, to control who can access the service. You also need to follow rate limits, which restrict how many requests you can make in a certain time. If you ignore these limits, your access may be blocked. Error handling is important too—you should check the HTTP status codes to understand if a request failed and handle problems like incorrect inputs or server issues properly.
